questions.question: Read the Iris dataset, which consists of 150 samples from three different species of Iris flowers (Iris setosa, Iris versicolor, and Iris virginica). Each sample is described by four features: the length and width of the sepals and petals. Using bagging and boosting technique, classify the Iris dataset into its three species. Provide a short explanation of your approach (approx. 100-150 words).

The Wine dataset contains 178 samples from three different wine varieties. Each sample is described by 13 features, such as color, odor, and skin area. Using bagging and boosting technique, classify the Wine dataset into its three wine varieties. Compare the performance of random forest with its base classifier and discuss about the results.

Suppose you have a binary classification problem where you have a dataset of 1000 samples and each sample is described by 10 features. You want to use bagging and boosting technique to classify the dataset. What are the advantages and disadvantages of using bagging and boosting on this problem? Also, suggest a few techniques to improve the performance of bagging and boosting on this dataset?
